manifest.title: TradeZella capability benchmark and Hermes parity roadmap
manifest.description: A source-backed translation of TradeZella's user benefits into an original, local-first Hermes roadmap.

cards.description: Exact roadmap-domain counts; these are classifications, not feature-coverage percentages.

charts.title: Every benchmark domain has an explicit disposition
charts.subtitle: Counts across 20 benefit domains; equal counts do not imply equal effort, value, or completeness.
charts.headerMarkdown: Six domains are shipped, six remain local priorities, six require a funded human gate, and two are deliberate non-goals.

tables.title: Recommended local-first delivery sequence
tables.subtitle: Six active local priorities, ordered by product leverage and readiness rather than estimated revenue.

query.description: Returns the exact post-build headline counts from the reviewed capability ledger.
query.sql: WITH ledger_summary(domains, shipped, local_priorities, gated_or_excluded) AS (VALUES (20, 6, 6, 8)) SELECT domains, shipped, local_priorities, gated_or_excluded FROM ledger_summary;
filters: All 20 reviewed capability domains as of 2026-07-17; no sampling or omitted dispositions.
metric_definitions: Capability domains = all unique ledger capability IDs.
Shipped domains = capability domains dispositioned shipped.
Local priorities = capability domains dispositioned prioritize-local.
Gated or excluded = gated-funded plus intentional-non-goal domains.

query.description: Returns the exact post-build disposition counts from the reviewed capability ledger.
query.sql: WITH ledger_counts(disposition, count) AS (VALUES ('shipped', 6), ('prioritize-local', 6), ('gated-funded', 6), ('intentional-non-goal', 2)) SELECT disposition, count FROM ledger_counts ORDER BY CASE disposition WHEN 'shipped' THEN 1 WHEN 'prioritize-local' THEN 2 WHEN 'gated-funded' THEN 3 WHEN 'intentional-non-goal' THEN 4 ELSE 5 END;
filters: All 20 reviewed capability domains as of 2026-07-17; no sampling or omitted dispositions.
metric_definitions: Count = number of unique capability domains assigned to the displayed disposition.

query.description: Returns the six active local-first increments and their gates and success evidence.
query.sql: WITH priority_roadmap(sequence, capability, first_increment, decision_gate, success_metric) AS (VALUES (1, 'Guided activation and account overview', 'Moderate the shipped manual and generic-CSV paths as separate first-use cohorts through exact stable-ID continuation; CSV participants must reconcile the immutable receipt before linked review.', 'No identity inference, account mutation, financial aggregation, or advisory onboarding copy.', 'In separate five-participant cohorts, at least four manual participants reach linked review through exact account scope and four CSV participants reach it from the reconciled canonical-scope-ordered receipt guide, without correction.'), (2, 'Reusable filters and saved views', 'Specify and ship device-local view presets only after protected preference ownership, reset, stale-value, lifecycle, and archive/export-exclusion rules are pinned.', 'No plaintext WebView localStorage downgrade; a saved scope cannot silently change governed report membership.', 'A preset restores exact account, date, search, and facet state after relaunch and fails closed on stale values.'), (3, 'Named broker CSV parser packs', 'Implement the first parser only after an anonymized real-format fixture and timezone, fee, identity, and asset semantics exist.', 'No guessed format, credential storage, silent fallback, or broader asset claim.', 'Golden files reconcile every row; repeat imports are idempotent and changed payloads fail visibly.'), (4, 'Playbook and template management', 'Add standalone local CRUD for reusable playbooks, ordered rules, and user-authored reflection templates.', 'Templates never auto-classify, overwrite evidence, or imply causality.', 'Create, edit, apply, export, and restore reproduce exact ordered content and explicit application evidence.'), (5, 'Deeper observational reports', 'Resolve Symbol Breakdown eligibility first; define each later timing, drawdown, or comparison family separately.', 'Every report needs a version, checksum, conservation, exclusions, restore equality, and exact drill-down.', 'Each published report reproduces from the current ledger and accounts for every eligible or excluded record.'), (6, 'Native data lifecycle and attachments', 'Complete Mac and iPhone acceptance before attachments, native restore claims, or approved Delete All Data.', 'Human approval for destructive deletion; device evidence for SQLCipher, Keychain, Files, photos, interruption, and accessibility.', 'Export, empty-device restore, continued write, re-export, attachment lifecycle, and approved deletion reconcile exact device state.')) SELECT sequence, capability, first_increment, decision_gate, success_metric FROM priority_roadmap ORDER BY sequence;
filters: Disposition = prioritize-local; six reviewed domains; explicit product sequence; no measured lift claim.

blocks.body: # TradeZella capability benchmark and Hermes parity roadmap

## Executive Summary

- **Why TradeZella appears popular:** it closes the recurring capture → review → insight → practice/accountability loop and presents that loop alongside education, support, creator reach, affiliates, and social proof. Public evidence supports awareness and satisfaction, not which factor causes acquisition, retention, or better trading outcomes.
- **What Hermes should copy:** the user benefits, not the product. Preserve Hermes's original iPhone-first, local-first identity; deepen its trustworthy review loop; and keep order execution, advice, and public profit competition outside the boundary.
- **What this milestone ships:** exact **Import Receipt Reconciliation**, **Daily Reflection Rhythm**, **Guided Account Overview**, **Manual Capture Review Continuation**, and **Generic CSV Receipt Review Continuation** as derived/projection slices, plus write-capable **Daily Reflection Rhythm Continuation**, all covered by regression and end-to-end evidence. The newest slice turns an exact missing or draft rhythm row into the existing locked-date Daily Journal action while completed, future, and demo rows remain read-only. The CSV slice adds one coherent read-only store query. A separate SQLite exact-replay parity correction changes existing write-path deduplication to match Session revision identity; no schema, receipt fact, financial formula, report cohort, network boundary, or native-readiness claim changes.
- **What happens next:** run separate five-participant manual and CSV cohorts; neither human result is currently available. Credentialed sync, licensed replay, hosted user-confirmed automation AI, structured education, mentorship/community, and commercial packaging stay behind explicit funded human gates.

## What appears to make TradeZella popular

1. **One coherent workflow.** Official onboarding ties import, dashboard, daily journal, trade log, notebook, reports, strategies, replay, progress tracking, backtesting, mentor access, and education into one recurring routine ([getting started](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/13863136-getting-started-with-tradezella)). Current July 2026 plans bundle reports, running P/L and drawdown, replay, backtesting, AI, PropFirm Sync, mentorship, Spaces, and community at different limits ([current pricing](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/8911582-our-pricing)). Zella AI's current guide includes user-triggered journal mutations—not only retrieval—such as tags, strategies, rules, checklists, notes, reviews, and background agents ([AI guide](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/11201153-what-is-zella-ai-tradezella-s-ai-trading-assistant)). TradeZella also claims 100K+ traders, 20.2B trades journaled, and 500+ broker connections; these are unaudited first-party scale claims ([homepage](https://www.tradezella.com/)).
2. **Fast visual feedback and drill-down.** The official product documents dashboard widgets, broad filters, report families with documented definitions, trade detail, strategies, and progress tracking that make a large execution history easier to interrogate ([reports](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/5829394-reports-overview-section), [filters](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/12417670-using-filters-in-tradezella), [progress tracker](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/10352042-getting-started-with-the-progress-tracker)). The July 7 changelog says active filters persist across login and refresh, while the filter guide still describes named favorite combinations as coming soon; persistence is confirmed, reusable named presets are not ([changelog](https://tradezella.canny.io/changelog)).
3. **Review plus deliberate practice.** Replay and current backtesting connect a recorded decision to later practice. Automated Backtesting compiles natural-language strategies into editable rules and exposes generated trades for chart inspection; manual Scenarios combine context, practice periods, rules, and preparation checklists ([Replay 2.0](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/7898284-trade-replay-2-0-a-powerful-tool-for-analyzing-trades-second-by-second), [automated backtesting](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/15934576-how-to-use-automated-backtesting-in-tradezella), [manual Scenarios](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/15934478-how-to-backtest-manually-in-tradezella)).
4. **Support is part of the experience.** Trustpilot displayed **4.8/5 from 978 reviews** on July 17, 2026, including **540 reviews in the prior 12 months**. Its AI-generated summary evaluated **534 recent reviews** and highlighted support, ease of journaling, metrics, reporting, and habit tracking. This is directional evidence only: TradeZella invites reviews, pays for a Trustpilot subscription, and the review population is not a representative customer survey ([Trustpilot](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/tradezella.com)).
5. **Large distribution surfaces.** Semrush estimated **1.09M all-device site visits in May 2026** and attributed 75.5% of estimated desktop traffic to Direct; visits are not users or subscribers ([Semrush](https://www.semrush.com/website/tradezella.com/overview/)). TradeZella also runs a recurring affiliate program for creators and educators, so some endorsements may be financially motivated ([partner program](https://help.tradezella.com/en/articles/12467249-tradezella-partner-program)).

**Evidence strength:** high that official sources document an integrated review loop and approachable analytics; high that public review sentiment and estimated traffic were strong on the observed dates; high that an affiliate distribution mechanism exists, but unknown whether it materially contributes to acquisition; no public evidence establishes which factor causes popularity, retention, profitability, or trader outcomes.

## Hermes's defensible translation

Hermes already owns the core journal loop: credential-free manual/CSV capture, immutable execution facts, exact projections, versioned trade and day reviews, a review queue, dashboard/calendar continuation, eight governed reports, and local export/empty-journal restore. Its differentiation is **trustworthy evidence, local privacy, and phone-first continuity**, not a cheaper copy of a web subscription.

The six new slices strengthen that position:

- **Import Receipt Reconciliation** exposes source, accepted, rejected, skipped, new-or-restored, already-present, and warning counts from the immutable receipt. It fixes a misleading accepted-row-as-execution label, preserves exact rollback focus, and removes a pre-existing rollback action from fictional demo data. Raw row content and issue messages remain out of scope because they are not available through both store adapters.
- **Daily Reflection Rhythm** classifies the latest seven trading sessions from calendar dates and current daily heads as completed, draft, or missing, then computes the maximal completed suffix. It ignores P&L, trade outcomes, trade-review state, note content, tags, emotion, and self-reported process score; no-trade reflections remain separate.
- **Guided Account Overview** lists every retained account with its current derived-trade count, then resolves one stable ledger ID into the existing all-activity Trade Browser scope. It clears temporary filters, preserves whole-workspace Dashboard/Report totals, and fails visibly on stale or tampered identity. It persists no activation progress and adds no account-level financial metric.
- **Manual Capture Review Continuation** re-derives one exact current trade—or both exact subjects bearing an AUTO reversal's immutable allocation fragments—from the confirmed manual execution identity, opens the existing stable-ID all-activity account scope, and offers state-qualified review actions without guessing from symbol, label, time, or recency. A later execution may have closed the reversal entry subject without invalidating recovery. A guide-origin save rebuilds the exact target and returns to the guide; an ordinary card save keeps the guide but returns normal screen focus. Visible guidance consumes only outcome and execution ID, while pending recovery temporarily retains the existing submission ID solely for acknowledgement. Its injected destination-render failure proof retries continuation with zero second commit reads.
- **Generic CSV Receipt Review Continuation** reads one exact active receipt plus accepted-row occurrence identities and a coherent ledger, conserves multiplicity before deduplication, and orders only linked current subjects against canonical account scope. A More guide pages ten targets without changing Trade Browser filters or auto-opening review. Known-positive recovery names the committed file/account/time, hides capture, cannot be dismissed, and performs zero file rereads, preparations, or recommits. Demo and rolled-back receipts are non-actionable; stock-only semantics and no-relaunch claims remain explicit. SQLite replay matching was separately hardened to source-name-aware, committed, unambiguous revision parity with Session.
- **Daily Reflection Rhythm Continuation** resolves an exact local missing or draft latest-seven row into the unchanged Daily Journal editor with the trading-session date locked. It rechecks unique DOM structure, displayed state, action kind, and exact saved-head identity before opening; completed, future, and demo rows remain read-only. Confirmed saves rebuild and focus that same row without repeating the write.

## Release evidence for the local sequence

1. **Guided activation and account overview** — The read-only stable-ID overview plus exact manual and generic-CSV capture-to-review continuations pass automated Linux/SQL.js/Chromium acceptance. CSV evidence covers immutable occurrence conservation, canonical linked-target order, fixed-size paging, guide-origin return, new-capture clearing, rollback supersession, adapter replay parity, and known-positive read/render recovery with zero reread, reprepare, or recommit. Both separate five-participant cohorts remain NOT RUN; hold account mutation, financial aggregation, advisory copy, broader CSV semantics, relaunch/native claims, and any activation-lift claim.
2. **Reusable filters and saved views** — Private preset labels have no approved protected preference owner. Release only after an encrypted adapter or schema/migration plus lifecycle and archive/export-exclusion rules are approved; plaintext WebView localStorage is not acceptable. A saved scope may not silently change governed report membership.
3. **Named broker CSV parser packs** — No guessed format, credential storage, silent fallback, or broader asset claim. Release only when golden files reconcile every row, repeats are idempotent, and changed payloads fail visibly.
4. **Playbook and template management** — Templates may not auto-classify, overwrite evidence, or imply causality. Release when create, edit, apply, export, and restore reproduce exact ordered content and explicit application evidence.
5. **Deeper observational reports** — Every report needs a version, checksum, conservation, exclusions, restore equality, and exact drill-down. Release each definition only when every eligible or excluded record is accounted for.
6. **Native data lifecycle and attachments** — Destructive deletion needs human approval; SQLCipher, Keychain, Files, photos, interruption, and accessibility need physical-device evidence. Release only after export, empty-device restore, continued write, re-export, attachment lifecycle, and approved deletion reconcile exact state.

## Human gates and deliberate exclusions

**Funded/gated:** commercial packaging and public price; read-only broker/prop-firm sync; licensed replay; reproducible backtesting; opt-in private reflection and user-confirmed automation AI; structured education, mentor/community, and selective sharing. Each needs its own rights, privacy, security, cost, support, deletion, and acceptance evidence. Education also requires curriculum ownership, content rights, versioned maintenance, accessibility, and explicit education-not-advice boundaries; any AI mutation requires preview, confirmation, reversibility, and model/version provenance. Broker sync remains read-only and may never imply an order-write method.

**Local but separately gated:** protected saved-view preference ownership, native SQLCipher/Keychain and Files acceptance, attachments, approved Delete All Data, generic-CSV asset-class semantics, atomic batch receipt recovery, Symbol Breakdown eligibility, and any new financial/report definition. Chromium evidence cannot satisfy the Mac/iPhone gate.

**Intentional non-goals:** live order execution, custody, signals, personalized advice, and public P&L leaderboards or profit-driven engagement. These conflict with Hermes's reflective trust and incentive boundary.

## Measurement plan

Measure the benefit loop without collecting journal content or promising outcomes:

- **Time to trusted first review — NOT RUN:** use separate five-participant fictional-data cohorts. At least four manual participants must reach linked current review through exact stable-ID account scope, and four generic-CSV participants must reach it from immutable-receipt reconciliation through the canonical-scope-ordered linked guide, without facilitator correction. Record elapsed time, self-corrections, and failure points without pooling branches or claiming retention or causality.
- **Import trust:** deterministic fixture conservation, duplicate idempotency, rollback/restore equality, and visible reconciliation—not telemetry from private executions.
- **Reflection continuity:** exact completed/draft/missing projection, current-run correctness, successful exact-row continuation to the locked-date existing editor, and return focus to the rebuilt row. Do not reward trading frequency, risk, or P&L.
- **Insight auditability:** every governed report conserves its eligible/excluded cohort and links to bounded stable-ID evidence.
- **Reliability and accessibility:** visible failure recovery, zero unintended external requests, 320/421px at 200% text, keyboard/focus proof, then VoiceOver/Dynamic Type/lifecycle/physical-device acceptance on Mac and iPhone.
- **Business viability:** owner-reviewed willingness-to-pay, support load, refund behavior, provider costs, and retention before any public price or recurring service decision.

## Evidence limits

No public source found a representative paid-user count, MAU/DAU, conversion rate, churn, retention cohort, feature adoption, sync-success rate, support SLA, or causal outcome study. TradeZella's own feature pages are authoritative for advertised capability, not effectiveness. Trustpilot, Semrush, Social Blade, Reddit, comparison sites, and affiliate material are directional and subject to selection, estimation, commercial, or anecdotal bias.

The disposition chart is a **decision taxonomy count**, not a feature-completion score. A shipped domain can still have explicit native holds; a gated domain may be strategically valuable but unauthorized or unfunded. Reverify all competitor pricing, plan limits, security statements, and comparative claims immediately before any external publication.

disposition_summary.definition: Implemented in current Hermes source with Linux/browser evidence; native claims remain gated.
disposition_summary.next_action: Protect invariants and complete full/native verification.

disposition_summary.definition: High-value work that can preserve the local-first deterministic boundary.
disposition_summary.next_action: Deliver the bounded sequence in the roadmap table.

disposition_summary.definition: Requires explicit rights, security, privacy, commercial, moderation, or recurring-cost approval.
disposition_summary.next_action: Hold implementation until the named human gate is satisfied.

disposition_summary.definition: Excluded because it conflicts with Hermes's product, safety, or incentive boundary.
disposition_summary.next_action: Keep the dependency and product boundary auditable.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Moderate the shipped manual and generic-CSV paths as separate first-use cohorts through exact stable-ID continuation; CSV participants must reconcile the immutable receipt before linked review.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: No identity inference, account mutation, financial aggregation, or advisory onboarding copy.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: In separate five-participant cohorts, at least four manual participants reach linked review through exact account scope and four CSV participants reach it from the reconciled canonical-scope-ordered receipt guide, without correction.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Specify and ship device-local view presets only after protected preference ownership, reset, stale-value, lifecycle, and archive/export-exclusion rules are pinned.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: No plaintext WebView localStorage downgrade; a saved scope cannot silently change governed report membership.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: A preset restores exact account, date, search, and facet state after relaunch and fails closed on stale values.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Implement the first parser only after an anonymized real-format fixture and timezone, fee, identity, and asset semantics exist.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: No guessed format, credential storage, silent fallback, or broader asset claim.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: Golden files reconcile every row; repeat imports are idempotent and changed payloads fail visibly.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Add standalone local CRUD for reusable playbooks, ordered rules, and user-authored reflection templates.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: Templates never auto-classify, overwrite evidence, or imply causality.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: Create, edit, apply, export, and restore reproduce exact ordered content and explicit application evidence.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Resolve Symbol Breakdown eligibility first; define each later timing, drawdown, or comparison family separately.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: Every report needs a version, checksum, conservation, exclusions, restore equality, and exact drill-down.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: Each published report reproduces from the current ledger and accounts for every eligible or excluded record.

priority_roadmap.first_increment: Complete Mac and iPhone acceptance before attachments, native restore claims, or approved Delete All Data.
priority_roadmap.decision_gate: Human approval for destructive deletion; device evidence for SQLCipher, Keychain, Files, photos, interruption, and accessibility.
priority_roadmap.success_metric: Export, empty-device restore, continued write, re-export, attachment lifecycle, and approved deletion reconcile exact device state.

sources.label: TradeZella July 2026 pricing and feature limits
